title: Terri-Lee Oleniuk
content:
People.ContactEmail tloleniuk@mccarthy.ca
- University of Saskatchewan
- Osgoode Hall Law School
A trusted advisor on complex regulatory and project approval matters, Terri-Lee helps clients advance major energy and infrastructure projects by navigating intersecting regulatory, environmental and Indigenous law considerations.
Terri-Lee Oleniuk is a Partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s Business Law Group in the Calgary office. She advises clients on project-related issues arising from natural resource development, with a focus on regulatory, environmental and Indigenous law matters that affect large-scale energy and infrastructure projects across Canada.
Terri-Lee works closely with proponents, developers and investors to help them secure regulatory approvals and manage risk throughout the lifecycle of complex projects. Her practice spans a broad range of sectors, including oil and gas, pipelines, liquefied natural gas, mining, midstream and petrochemicals, as well as electricity transmission, hydroelectric, solar, wind and battery energy storage projects. In recent years, her regulatory work has been highly active in the power market, reflecting the evolving landscape of energy transition and generation development.
A central aspect of Terri-Lee’s practice involves representing clients in environmental assessments and regulatory proceedings before administrative tribunals. She regularly acts in matters involving tolls and tariffs, regulatory investigations and inquiries, and advises clients on environmental, social and governance considerations that arise in connection with major project development and operations. Terri-Lee also advises on, and negotiates, a wide range of agreements with Indigenous groups, helping clients address consultation and partnership considerations in a practical and commercially informed manner.
Terri-Lee is admitted to the bars of Alberta, British Columbia and the Northwest Territories. She received a BSc (Physiology) and a JD (with distinction) from the University of Saskatchewan, and an LLM in Energy and Infrastructure Law from Osgoode Hall Law School.
